description: OrderedHashTableObject Rehash — size_t Allocation Size Truncated to uint32_t in AllocNurseryOrMallocBuffer, memcpy Writes Past Under-Allocated Buffer (CVE-2025-49710). CVE-2025-49710 (sec-high). The OrderedHashTableObject code uses size_t for buffer size calculations but calls AllocNurseryOrMallocBuffer which takes a uint32_t argument. When a Map or Set grows large enough (SpiderMonkey allows up to ~170 million entries, far exceeding Chrome's 2^24 limit), the allocation size overflows on truncation to uint32_t, causing a too-small buffer to be allocated. The subsequent memcpy during rehash writes past the allocated buffer, enabling heap corruption. The fix limits the maximum number of Map/Set entries and transitions allocation functions to use size_t.

fixes.description: Bug 1970095 - Clean up some Map/Set code. r=iain
